plan: Trim SPY to 20% to meet concentration limits and reduce broad market exposure.
Sell all SOXL and SPAL to eliminate leveraged ETF risk, aligning with capital preservation.
Reduce QQQI and QQQ exposure due to their downtrend status and to free up capital.
Initiate a new position in MSFT, a high-quality name, for stable growth.
Maintain a significant cash buffer by reducing overall equity exposure.
This strategy prioritizes capital preservation and reduces overall portfolio volatility.
conservative.read: This conservative approach targets a modest ~1.5%/month, focusing on capital preservation. Risk is significantly reduced by eliminating leveraged ETFs and lowering overall exposure. Expect lower drawdowns but also slower growth potential.

moves.note: Exit SOXL due to below stop
Exit SPAL due to below stop

plan: Trim SPY to 25% to align with concentration limits and balance broad market exposure.
Sell SOXL and SPAL to remove positions below stop and reduce leveraged ETF risk.
Reduce QQQI and QQQ exposure due to their downtrend status, reallocating capital.
Buy NVDU and MSFT, leveraging high-score scanner setups for quality tech growth.
Maintain a cash buffer of approximately 10% to manage market fluctuations.
This plan balances growth potential with risk management, focusing on quality.
balanced.read: This balanced approach aims for ~2.5%/month by blending growth and risk management. It reduces leverage and concentration while adding quality tech names. Expect moderate drawdowns with improved growth prospects compared to conservative.

plan: Trim SPY to 25% to comply with concentration limits, freeing capital for high-conviction plays.
Sell SOXL and SPAL to exit positions below stop and reallocate capital to stronger setups.
Reduce QQQI and QQQ exposure significantly due to their downtrends, focusing on uptrends.
Buy NVDU and MSFT, concentrating in these high-scoring scanner setups for aggressive growth.
Maintain a small cash buffer, deploying most capital into high-potential trades.
Actively watch SQQQ for potential short-term hedging or opportunistic plays if market turns.
aggressive.read: This aggressive approach targets ~4%/month by concentrating in high-scoring setups and leveraging exposure. It carries significantly higher risk, including larger potential drawdowns and the daily decay/rebalancing of leveraged instruments. Strict stop discipline is crucial to manage these amplified risks.

plan: Trim SPY to 20% to meet concentration limits and reallocate towards income-generating assets.
Sell all SOXL and SPAL to eliminate leveraged ETF risk, prioritizing stability over growth.
Increase QQQI exposure significantly to boost monthly distribution potential.
Sell QQQ, SMH, and CRWD to reduce high-beta exposure and focus on income and stability.
Maintain a substantial cash buffer, prioritizing low drawdown and reliable income.
This strategy aims to generate steady monthly distributions with minimal growth focus.
income.read: This income-focused approach targets ~1%/month through distributions, prioritizing stability. It significantly reduces growth-oriented and high-beta holdings. While aiming for reliable income, distributions are variable, and NAV can still decline in market downturns; this is not a savings account.
